w:r.w:t: Professional Networking Platform for Education, Research & Institutions

Link and Learn is a purpose-built web-based professional networking platform for India's education, research, and institutional sector. Developed to bridge the significant gap left by general-purpose platforms such as LinkedIn and Facebook, the platform unifies students, faculty, researchers, institutions, government bodies, NGOs, funding agencies, and vendors within a single, governed ecosystem.

This Business Solution Document (BSD) translates the requirements captured in the BRD v1.0 into a structured solution design — covering the system architecture, role-based access model, module specifications, data flows, and the deployment roadmap aligned to the three-phase delivery timeline.

The solution is anchored on a ReactJS front end with a PHP + MySQL backend, ensuring rapid development velocity, full control over data, and minimal infrastructure overhead within the 12-week internship delivery window.
The table below maps each identified business problem to the corresponding platform solution delivered by Link and Learn.
No central, verified space for academics to publish and discover research work
Journals & Articles module with rich-text editor, tagging, and AI-powered authenticity detection
Fragmented, inefficient institutional recruitment via email and notice boards
Structured recruitment module with auto-matching push notifications to eligible candidates
No structured marketplace for institutional procurement of facility items
Vendor Connector marketplace with procurement postings, quote submissions, and facility dashboard
Academic credentials cannot be verified digitally in real time
Degree certificate upload and admin-reviewed verification workflow with verified badge on profile
Events and conferences managed through uncoordinated channels
End-to-end event management: creation, online registration, QR ticketing, and automated reminders
Vendor and staff contact numbers exposed during procurement, creating privacy risk
Call masking architecture — platform-routed contact with direct numbers never surfaced in the UI
The Link and Learn platform follows a Single Page Application (SPA) architecture with a PHP REST API backend and MySQL database. The layered architecture comprises:
Presentation Layer: ReactJS + Vite SPA, styled with Tailwind CSS. Fully responsive across mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports.
Business Logic Layer: React feature modules and PHP server-side scripts for server-side operations (AI detection, certificate validation triggers, recruitment matching).
Data Layer: MySQL relational database for all platform data; server-side file storage for file assets (certificates, profile media, ad creatives).
Authentication & Security Layer: PHP session-based and JWT authentication with role-based RBAC; TLS 1.2+ encryption for all data in transit; bcrypt for password hashing.
Notification Layer: Third-party push notification service (e.g., OneSignal) for real-time push notifications across job alerts, event reminders, and recruitment matches.
AI Integration Layer: Hugging Face API for AI-generated content detection and plagiarism analysis — invoked via PHP server-side scripts on article submission.
Core SPA framework and build tooling
JWT-based login, role provisioning via PHP
Relational database for all platform data
Degree certificates, media, ad creatives stored on server
AI detection triggers, cert validation, recruitment matching
Job alerts, event reminders, recruitment matches via push service
Source code management and CI collaboration
This section provides the solution design for each functional module, aligned to the Functional Requirements (FR) defined in BRD v1.0.
The authentication solution provides two distinct registration and login pathways — Personal and Institutional — each with a unique profile schema, verification workflow, and access control scope.
Email/password registration via PHP authentication API. Users select an account sub-type (Student or Researcher/Faculty) at registration. JWT tokens are issued on login and expire within 24 hours.

Institutional accounts complete an extended registration form (organisation name, type, affiliation documentation). Accounts are placed in a Pending state and require Platform Admin approval before activation.

User roles are stored in the MySQL users table and encoded in JWT tokens. React Router enforces role-based route protection on the frontend. PHP middleware validates roles on sensitive server-side operations.
JWT tokens are issued and refreshed via PHP API. Tokens expire in 24 hours per NFR-03. Forced re-authentication on sensitive operations (cert upload, admin actions).
Passwords hashed with bcrypt. All communication over TLS 1.2+. Admin accounts are provisioned internally — no self-registration permitted.
Personal users receive a LinkedIn-style profile with real-time editing and an in-browser PDF resume generator.
Bio, skills, education history, work experience, publications, certificates. All sections editable in real time with auto-save to the MySQL database via PHP API.
Built with react-pdf library. Generates in-browser from current profile data. Minimum two templates available. Download triggered on demand — no server-side processing required.
Institutional accounts have a separate profile schema: organisation name, type, departments, verified badge, linked personal users (faculty/students). Displayed as a public group page.
Personal profiles are publicly discoverable by default. Privacy controls allow users to restrict visibility of specific sections.
A manual upload-and-review workflow provides credential verification in v1.0, with a verified badge displayed on approved profiles.
Users upload PDF or image files via PHP file upload handler to server storage. File size limit enforced client-side. Upload triggers a MySQL record with status: Pending.
Platform Admin panel surfaces all Pending certificates sorted by submission date. Admin reviews document, then approves (sets status: Verified) or rejects (sets status: Rejected + reason).
On approval, a Verified badge appears on the user's profile. Badge is stored as a MySQL flag — tampering protected by PHP server-side role checks.
SLA target: badge appears within 48 hours of admin approval. Pending certificate status is visible to the user throughout.
A rich-text publishing system for research articles and institutional knowledge, discoverable via full-text search.
Rich-text editor supporting headings, lists, hyperlinks, and image embeds. Implemented via a React WYSIWYG library (e.g., Quill or TipTap).
Articles tagged by category, topic, and author. MySQL full-text and indexed queries power title/author/tag search. Articles indexed and surfaced in the platform feed and author profile.

On submission, article content is passed to the AI detection Cloud Function (FR-05). Article is held in a Draft/Pending state until the detection API returns. If flagged, the article is withheld and sent to admin review.
Students and the public can read articles. Researcher/Faculty and institutional accounts (Govt Body) can publish.
An automated pipeline detects AI-generated content, plagiarism, and fake profile signals on article submission.
PHP script triggered on article submission to MySQL. Extracts article text and dispatches to Hugging Face detection API.
API returns a confidence score (0–1) and detection category (AI-generated, plagiarised, clean). Score and category stored against the article record in MySQL.
Articles with AI-generated confidence score above a configurable threshold (e.g., 0.75) are automatically hidden and placed in the Admin moderation queue.
Every detection result stored with: timestamp, model version, confidence score, article ID, and subsequent admin reviewer decision — satisfying NFR-07.
Platform Admin reviews flagged articles, views the AI score, and can approve (publish) or permanently reject (remove) the article with a recorded reason.
Structured job and vacancy management for institutions, with automated candidate matching and push notifications.
Institution posts a vacancy with: role type (faculty/PhD/research/staff), eligibility criteria (qualifications, experience, discipline), deadline, department, and description.

On vacancy creation, a PHP background script queries MySQL for personal users (Students, Researcher/Faculty) whose profile criteria match the eligibility fields. Matching runs within 1 hour of posting.
Matching candidates receive a push notification (via OneSignal) with role title, institution name, and a deep link to the vacancy. Non-matching users are not notified.
Applicants submit via the platform. Both the institution (in their HR dashboard) and the applicant (in their applications panel) can track status: Applied, Under Review, Shortlisted, Rejected.
PhD and research programme openings use the same posting flow with a dedicated CFA template and extended eligibility fields (research area, supervisor availability).
A complete event lifecycle solution from creation through registration to QR-based attendance management.
Institutions and personal users create events with: title, date/time, location, description, category, and capacity limit. Events published to a discoverable events feed.
Attendees register via a platform registration form. MySQL tracks registered attendees against event capacity. Capacity enforcement prevents over-registration.
On registration, a unique QR code is generated client-side (encoding event ID + attendee ID) and delivered in-app and via notification. Can be screenshotted for offline use.
Push notifications dispatched via PHP scheduled scripts (cron jobs): 24 hours before and 1 hour before the event start time, to all registered attendees.
Event organiser uses the platform to scan QR tickets at the venue. Attendance marked in MySQL in real time.
Real-time in-app messaging with a call masking architecture that protects direct contact numbers of all parties.
MySQL-backed one-to-one and group messaging with WebSocket or polling. Messages stored in a conversations table. Real-time delivery handled via WebSocket (e.g., Ratchet/Pusher) or long-polling.
New message events trigger push notifications (via OneSignal) to the recipient if not actively in the conversation view.

All contact buttons in the platform surface the platform company proxy number only. Direct vendor and institution numbers are stored in MySQL with PHP role-based access controls — never returned to the client UI.
Live telephony routing via Exotel/Twilio is a v2.0 feature. In v1.0, the UI design and masked number display are complete; the routing API connection is deferred.
A structured marketplace connecting institutions' procurement needs with verified vendor suppliers.
Institutions post requirements with: item type, quantity, budget range, deadline, and delivery location. Requirements are visible to all registered vendors.
Vendors maintain a product/service catalogue in their profile: categories, descriptions, pricing range, and service areas.
Vendors browse open requirements and submit quotes directly through the platform. Each quote records price, timeline, and terms.
Institution dashboard tracks all procurement activity: Open Requirements, Quotes Received, In-Progress Orders, Completed Tasks, and vendor interaction history.
All vendor-institution communication routed through platform masked messaging. Direct numbers never surfaced (enforced by PHP server-side access controls).
A targeted advertising system serving relevant ad content to matched user segments across the platform.
Advertisers submit ad creative (image + copy) with targeting parameters: user role, institution type, and geographic region.
All ads require Platform Admin review and approval before going live. Admin can approve, reject, or request changes.
Sponsored posts injected into the professional feed at defined intervals for matching user segments. Banner ads served on relevant pages based on targeting parameters.
Real-time impression count and click-through rate (CTR) tracked in MySQL. Advertiser analytics dashboard shows campaign performance metrics.
Ad targeting parameters cannot include personally identifiable information. Targeting is segment-level only (role, institution type, region).

Link and Learn operates on a strict Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) architecture. Eight distinct roles are defined across two login categories — Personal and Institutional — with a dedicated internal Platform Admin role. Every feature, data access point, and UI element is governed by the assigned role.
Educational institutions managed by designated admins
Government research agencies, councils, and laboratories
Suppliers of furniture, lab equipment, and services
Targeted ad placement on the platform
Platform Admin approval for each campaign

The matrix below defines access levels for each functional module by role. Legend: POST = create/post, READ = read only, APPLY = apply/register, SUBMIT = submit for review, REVIEW = admin moderation, QUOTE = submit vendor quotes, APPROVE = admin approval, MASKED = masked/restricted access.
The following sections define the complete capabilities, restrictions, and user journey for each of the eight platform roles.
Undergraduate, postgraduate, or doctoral students. The largest user segment on the platform.
Create a LinkedIn-style academic profile; generate a downloadable PDF resume from profile data on demand.
Upload certificates for admin verification; receive a Verified badge upon approval within 48 hours.
View, like, comment, and share posts from connections and followed institutions.
Browse and apply for PhD programmes, research roles, and student-relevant postings; track application status.
Discover events, register online, receive QR-code tickets, and automated reminders (24h + 1h before).
One-to-one and group in-app messaging; masked contact routing for vendor queries.
Connect with peers, faculty, and researchers; follow institution pages.
Browse and read published journals and research articles; cannot publish.
Cannot post institutional job vacancies, PhD calls, or procurement requirements.
Cannot approve or reject degree certificates — this is a Platform Admin action.
Cannot access the Platform Admin panel or any content moderation tools.
Cannot create vendor listings or submit procurement quotes.
Register with email/password → select Student personal account type.
Complete profile: bio, education, skills, experience, and publications sections.
Upload degree certificate → status shows Pending; badge awarded within 48 hours of admin approval.
Connect with peers and follow institutions → professional feed populates.
Receive push notifications for matching job/PhD listings based on profile criteria.
Register for events → receive QR ticket + automated reminder notifications.
Academic staff, postdoctoral researchers, and independent researchers who publish work, collaborate on grants, and apply for research roles.
Full access to all features available to the Student role, including profile, feed, applications, and events.
Publish research articles using the rich-text editor with categorisation, tagging, and full-text search indexing.
Submitted articles are automatically scanned for AI-generated content and plagiarism before publication.
Provide and receive connection-based endorsements; AI-assisted credibility scoring displayed on profile.
Search and connect with peers working in related research areas via advanced profile filters.
Receive push notifications for relevant funding calls posted by NGOs and government bodies.
Cannot post institutional job vacancies — institutional account required for posting.
Cannot manage institutional procurement requirements or vendor interactions.
Cannot access the Platform Admin moderation panel.
Register → select Researcher/Faculty personal account type.
Build comprehensive academic profile including publications list and research interests.
Publish journal or article → AI detection pipeline runs automatically on submission.
Engage with the research community via feed, comments, and direct messaging.
Receive notifications for grant calls and PhD supervision opportunities matching research areas.
Provide and receive reference endorsements through the connection network.
Educational institutions with admin accounts managed by designated institutional administrators.
Public profile page displaying institution name, type, departments, faculty affiliations, and verified institution badge.
Post faculty, staff, and research role vacancies; system auto-matches and notifies eligible candidates within 1 hour.
Publish PhD programme openings with eligibility criteria; push notifications dispatched to matching students and researchers.
Create and manage institutional events; handle online registration; issue QR-code tickets; send automated reminders.
Post facility procurement needs (furniture, lab equipment, infrastructure); receive and evaluate competitive vendor quotes.
Track all open, in-progress, and completed procurement tasks and vendor interaction history.
Communicate with vendors, applicants, and event registrants via masked platform routing.
Personal users affiliated with the institution can link their profile to the institution page.
Institutional account requires Platform Admin approval before activation.
Cannot approve or validate degree certificates — this is a Platform Admin action only.
Direct staff contact numbers must not be exposed in the UI — all contact via platform masking.
Payment for premium features is deferred to v2.0.
Register institutional account → submit for Platform Admin approval.
Upon approval: complete institution profile, add departments, branding, and contact information.
Post faculty or PhD vacancies → system auto-notifies matching candidates within 1 hour.
Create events → manage registrations and QR ticket issuance from events dashboard.
Post procurement requirements → receive and compare vendor quotes via the Vendor Connector.
Track all procurement and HR activities via the Facility Management dashboard.
Central and state government research agencies, councils, and laboratories participating in the national academic ecosystem.
Full access to all features available to School/College institutional accounts.
Post interdisciplinary research project calls open to faculty and researchers across institutions nationwide.
Publish grant calls with eligibility criteria, budget range, and application deadlines; matched researchers receive push notifications.
Share official research advisories, reports, and policy documents directly to the platform feed.
Organise national conferences, symposia, and workshops with high-capacity registration and QR attendance management.
Account requires Platform Admin approval and manual verification of government affiliation before activation.
Cannot process transactions or payments through the platform in v1.0.
Live telephony call masking deferred to v2.0.
Register → select Government Research Body institutional type.
Platform Admin manually verifies government affiliation before account activation.
Publish grant announcements and collaboration calls → matched users notified via push notifications.
Post large-scale events; manage registrations and QR-based attendance verification.
Engage with researchers through verified institutional messaging.
Non-governmental organisations and private funding agencies that publish grants, partner with institutions, and manage capacity-building events.
Verified NGO/funding agency profile with mission statement, funded research areas, and institutional badge.
Post funding opportunities with eligibility criteria, grant quantum, and application deadlines; researchers receive targeted push notifications.
Organise workshops, capacity-building programmes, and awareness events with online registration and QR ticketing.
Post procurement needs for programme materials, equipment, and operational services.
Connect with institutions and researchers for collaborative programme delivery via institutional messaging.
Cannot post academic job vacancies or PhD programme openings.
Payment integration for grant disbursements is deferred to v2.0.
Account requires Platform Admin approval before activation.
Register → select NGO/Funding Agency institutional type.
Platform Admin reviews and approves the account.
Publish funding calls → researchers and faculty receive targeted push notifications.
Organise and manage events with online registration and QR ticket issuance.
Post procurement requirements → engage with verified vendor suppliers.
Suppliers and service providers of furniture, lab equipment, infrastructure items, and educational materials to institutions.
Registered vendor listing with product/service catalogue, categories, pricing information, and service area.
Browse and filter all open procurement requirements posted by institutions across the platform.
Submit competitive quotes directly to institutions through the platform with price, timeline, and terms.
Communicate with institutions via platform-routed masked messaging; direct contact numbers never exposed in the UI.
Dashboard tracking status of submitted quotes: Pending, Accepted, In Progress, and Completed.
Place sponsored listings in the vendor marketplace to increase discoverability to procurement-active institutions.
Cannot access personal user profiles, connections, or social feed content.
Cannot post academic job vacancies, PhD calls, or research funding announcements.
Direct phone numbers are never shown to institutions — all contact via platform masking (enforced by PHP server-side access controls).
Cannot approve, reject, or modify institution procurement postings.
Platform Admin reviews and approves vendor account and catalogue.
Build product/service catalogue with descriptions, categories, and pricing ranges.
Browse open procurement requirements → filter by category, location, and budget.
Submit competitive quotes → communicate with institutions via masked messaging.
Track quote outcomes and active order status from the vendor dashboard.
Internal platform operators responsible for content moderation, account verification, and system-wide oversight.
Review, approve, or reject institutional account registration requests. Approve government body accounts with affiliation verification.
Review uploaded certificate documents; approve with Verified badge or reject with a documented reason. SLA: within 48 hours.
Review AI-flagged articles for AI-generated content, plagiarism, or fake profile signals; approve or permanently remove.
Act on AI-flagged fake profile signals; suspend or deactivate accounts with a recorded audit trail.
Review and approve advertiser ad submissions before going live; monitor impressions and CTR analytics.
View, suspend, reactivate, or permanently deactivate any user or institution account.
Access platform-wide engagement, registration, content moderation, and system health metrics.
Admin panel is accessible exclusively to Platform Admin accounts — separate route, elevated JWT role claim.
Admin accounts cannot be self-registered — provisioned internally by the platform operations team only.
Provisioned internally by the operations team — no self-registration.
Log in to the dedicated Admin Panel (separate route, elevated JWT role claim).
Process institution approval queue — review and action submissions daily.
Process certificate validation queue — approve or reject within the 48-hour SLA.
Review AI-flagged content queue — approve or remove within the defined moderation SLA.
Review and approve pending ad campaigns before they go live.
Monitor the analytics dashboard for platform health and engagement metrics.
Institutions, vendors, funding agencies, and publishers who place targeted advertisements on the platform.
Submit advertisement creative (image + copy) with audience targeting parameters (user role, institution type, geographic region).
Define target segments by user role, institution category, and geographic region. PII-free targeting only.
Place sponsored posts in the professional feed, served to matching user segments at defined feed intervals.
Purchase banner ad slots served across relevant platform pages based on audience targeting parameters.
Monitor real-time impression counts, click-through rates (CTR), and campaign performance metrics.
All ad submissions require Platform Admin review and approval before going live.
Targeting parameters cannot include personally identifiable information — segment-level only.
Payment for ad campaigns is deferred to v2.0.
Submit ad creative and audience targeting parameters via the advertiser portal.
Ad placed in Platform Admin review queue.
Platform Admin reviews and approves the ad.
Ad goes live and is served to matching user segments across the platform.
Advertiser monitors impressions and CTR via the analytics dashboard.
React code-splitting (lazy loading) per route. CDN-served static assets. MySQL indexed queries to avoid full-table scans.
PHP application server scales with horizontal deployment. MySQL handles concurrent reads/writes with proper indexing and connection pooling. Push notifications managed via OneSignal at scale.
PHP enforces TLS via server configuration. Passwords hashed with bcrypt in PHP. JWT tokens expire every 24 hours with refresh.
PHP middleware and MySQL role-based access controls restrict certificate and contact number reads to authorized roles only. Direct numbers excluded from all client-side API responses.
Hosted server SLA provides 99.95% uptime for core services. Maintenance windows communicated via in-platform notification 48h in advance.
Tailwind CSS responsive breakpoints applied throughout. QA testing across all three viewport sizes per sprint.
Detection results stored with timestamp, model version, score, reviewer decision
MySQL AI detection records include: article ID, submission timestamp, model version, confidence score, flag category, and admin reviewer decision with timestamp.
Full compliance: ReactJS + Vite SPA. PHP REST API, MySQL database, and server-side file storage are the backend infrastructure.
The platform is delivered across three phases over a 12-week internship timeline. Each phase produces a testable increment that is demonstrated to the project supervisor at the phase close.
Authentication (Personal + Institutional), Profile & Resume Creator, Professional Feed, Journals & Articles, PHP/MySQL backend setup
Working login + profile demo with RBAC enforced
Recruitment Module, Events Management, Vendor Connector, In-App Messaging, Auto Resume PDF Generator
Recruitment + Events live; Vendor quotes end-to-end
All roles active; Vendor Connector live; Facility Dashboard
AI Content Detection, Degree Certificate Validation, Reference Checking, Push Notifications (OneSignal), Platform Admin Panel, Advertisement Module
Full platform demo; all 10 FRs functional; supervisor sign-off
Platform Admin full panel; Advertiser portal; AI detection live
The Link and Learn v1.0 platform will be considered successfully delivered when all of the following criteria are verified by the project supervisor.
Both personal and institutional login types functional with RBAC enforced
QA login testing with all role types
User can create a full profile and download a correctly formatted PDF resume
Institution posts a vacancy and a matching user receives a push notification within 1 hour
Functional test with matching and non-matching profiles
Event created; user registers; receives QR ticket; reminder notification fires
AI-generated article withheld and placed in admin review queue with confidence score stored
Submit a known AI-generated article and verify the flag
Degree certificate approved by admin; verified badge appears on profile
Real-time messages delivered between two users without a page reload
Two-device messaging test with delivery time measurement
Vendor direct numbers not visible anywhere in the platform UI
UI audit across all vendor-facing pages
Application fully usable on 360px, 768px, and 1280px viewports
Responsive testing across all three breakpoints
All Phase 1, 2, and 3 modules functional and demonstrated to supervisor
Final platform demo and supervisor sign-off

Link and Learn is designed for sustainable revenue generation from platform launch through four primary streams. Payment gateway integration is a v2.0 feature; the pricing and commercial architecture below is designed and ready for activation.
Enhanced profile features, priority listing in search, and advanced analytics for institutional accounts
Schools, colleges, NGOs, government research bodies
Paid featured placement and priority ranking in the Vendor Connector marketplace
Equipment suppliers, furniture vendors, service providers
Fee per faculty job vacancy or PhD call-for-application posted by institutions
Institutions hiring faculty, PhD supervisors, research staff
Sponsored posts in the professional feed and banner ads served to matching user segments
Hugging Face API rate limits throttle AI detection during high submission volumes
Implement request queuing in PHP background jobs; batch submissions; implement retry with exponential backoff.
Server resource limits exceeded during load testing
Upgrade server resources (RAM, CPU, storage) proactively in Phase 2 to accommodate growing data and traffic.
Institutional account approvals create admin bottleneck at launch
Establish a daily review SLA for the admin; pre-approve known institutions before go-live.
Manual degree certificate validation creates a queue backlog post-launch
Implement email notifications to admin for new submissions; set 48-hour SLA and monitor queue size.
Call masking (UI only in v1.0) creates user confusion if numbers are expected
Clear UI copy stating "Contact via platform"; explicit note that direct numbers are not shared for privacy.
Scope creep from stakeholder requests during development
Maintain formal change control; all new requirements evaluated against the 12-week timeline and BRD scope.
Frontend must be built exclusively with ReactJS as mandated by the project brief. No alternative JavaScript frameworks permitted.
Platform must be delivered within the 12-week internship timeline across three defined phases.
Initial release is a responsive web application only. Native Android/iOS apps are deferred to a subsequent phase.
Live telephony integration for call masking (Exotel/Twilio) is deferred to v2.0 due to cost and third-party complexity.
Payment processing is out of scope for v1.0. All premium and transaction features activate in v2.0.
Platform launches in English only. Multi-language support is a future phase requirement.
AI detection features are dependent on Hugging Face API availability, rate limits, and response latency.
A PHP + MySQL stack serves as the primary backend, hosted on a web server for v1.0.
Project supervisor is available for weekly milestone reviews and provides sign-off within 3 business days of each deliverable.
Degree certificate validation in v1.0 is a manual admin review process. No DigiLocker or NAD government API integration is assumed.
Institutional accounts requiring verification will be manually approved by the Platform Admin during the early launch period.
Users are assumed to have a modern web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari — latest two versions) and a stable internet connection.
Reference checking in v1.0 operates on connection-based endorsements. AI-powered credibility scoring is a Phase 3 enhancement.
Business Solution Document — this document; translates BRD requirements into a structured technical and operational solution design.
Business Requirement Document — the source document (BRD v1.0) capturing platform requirements.
Role-Based Access Control — access rights assigned based on user roles (personal, institutional, platform admin).
JSON Web Token — compact, URL-safe token for secure authentication between client and server.
Push Notification Service — a third-party service (e.g., OneSignal) for real-time push notification delivery across the platform.
Single Page Application — a web application architecture where React manages routing client-side.
PHP Backend — a server-side PHP application providing authentication, database management via MySQL, file storage, and business logic on a self-hosted server.
Click-Through Rate — the ratio of ad clicks to impressions, used in advertiser analytics.
Third-party API (Hugging Face) integration analysing articles for AI-generated text, plagiarism, and fake profile signals.
Architecture where vendor/institution direct numbers are hidden; contact routes through the platform proxy number.
Quick Response code generated for an event registrant, used for attendance verification at the event venue.
The marketplace module where institutions post procurement requirements and vendors submit quotes.
Call-for-Applications — a specialised recruitment posting type for PhD programmes and research positions.
Functional Requirement — a specific capability the system must provide, as defined in BRD v1.0 (FR-01 through FR-10).
Non-Functional Requirement — system quality attributes (performance, security, availability) as defined in BRD v1.0.
Initial Business Solution Document — all sections authored
Link & Learn | Business Solution Document | v1.0 | April 2026 | Confidential
